opp.name: Tekni-Plex_ANA_Apr'26
opp.next_step: Share NDA with TekniPlex team (Cristian, Carlos, Gerardo) for signature. Send Zycus presentation and overview materials. Share implementation discovery checklist (data requirements, ERP integration inputs, supplier/spend data needs). Schedule follow-up meeting with Solution Consultant (Karson) post-NDA. Conduct deep-dive discovery session: Current ERP and procurement tech stack Spend categories (MRO, Capex, indirect) Supplier base and fragmentation Key pain points and improvement areas Build tailored business case: Savings potential (autonomous negotiation, tail spend, supplier consolidation) Efficiency gains and cycle time reduction Align on initial pilot scope (recommended: Autonomous Negotiation / tail spend category). Define implementation roadmap and timeline (2–3 months for initial module).
opp.ais_why:
• TekniPlex is in early stages of evaluating AI-driven procurement transformation with a focus on autonomous negotiation and AI-enabled sourcing. Direct match to ANA.
• Next step explicitly recommends an initial pilot scoped to Autonomous Negotiation on tail spend. Mutual alignment on agentic execution.
• Created late April 2026 with last activity mid-May and NDA workflow underway with Cristian, Carlos, and Gerardo. Active discovery cadence anchored to the AI use case.

SF NextStep (updated 5 Jun 2026): 'Build tailored business case: Savings potential (autonomous negotiation, tail spend, supplier consolidation) Efficiency gains and cycle time reduction'
metrics.narrative: Buyer articulated qualitative pain around process improvement, cost savings priority, and visibility gaps, but no quantified business case yet. On 29 Apr 2026 call, Carlos Velasquez stated procurement's priority is 'benefits in savings that we can receive if we implement this software' and confirmed focus on MRO, Capex, indirect spend categories. Kevin Cipollaro (Zycus) cited industry benchmarks of 10-15% savings via autonomous negotiation, supplier consolidation, and maverick-spend reduction, but Tekni-Plex has not yet validated these against their own spend baseline. Cristian Velasquez asked 'what will be the value of this implementation?' and requested discovery documentation to understand ROI for companies their size. The next step—building a tailored business case with savings potential and efficiency gains—is explicitly documented in NextStep as of 5 Jun 2026. Quantifying the value case is the immediate deliverable to move this deal forward.

sources: Avoma calls 19 May 2025, 23 May 2025: Gerardo Perez initiated and attended intro calls, requested module-focused follow-up
Avoma call 29 Apr 2026: Cristian Velasquez attended, asked evaluative questions, delegated coordination to Carlos/Gerardo
Avoma call 29 Apr 2026: Carlos Velasquez Garnica asked detailed technical questions but no advocacy evidence
SF OpportunityContactRole: Gerardo Perez (Evaluator), Cristian Velasquez (Decision Maker), Carlos Velasquez Garnica (Evaluator)
champion.narrative: No confirmed champion; Gerardo Perez is the most engaged contact but has not demonstrated advocacy or access to the Economic Buyer. Gerardo initiated contact (19 May 2025 intro call), rescheduled to accommodate his availability, attended the 23 May 2025 call, and requested a follow-up focused on e-sourcing and spend analysis modules with IT manager Daniel Diaz. However, Gerardo did not attend the 29 Apr 2026 call (only Cristian, Carlos, and Harold), and there is no evidence of him driving the agenda, introducing Zycus to senior stakeholders, or pushing back on competitors. His engagement has been reactive (responding to Zycus outreach) rather than proactive. Cristian Velasquez (Decision Maker per Contact Role, Sr Director Supply Chain) attended the 29 Apr 2026 call and asked substantive questions (implementation value, data requirements, company-size fit), but his engagement is evaluative, not advocacy. He delegated ongoing coordination to Carlos and Gerardo ('I'll leave that especially to Carlos to keep this discussion'), signaling he is the gate-keeper, not a coach or internal seller. Carlos Velasquez Garnica (Manager Procurement Capex, Evaluator) asked the most detailed technical questions on the 29 Apr call (supplier comparison logic, agent vs. tool, modular cost, multi-location support) and is clearly the technical evaluator, but there is no evidence he is championing Zycus internally. Developing a champion—someone with access to Cristian and the Economic Buyer who will advocate for Zycus and navigate internal politics—is a gap and a priority.

sources: SF Competitors__c: null
Avoma call 23 May 2025: Gerardo Perez, 'Can you share with us who you are using for Source To Pay?' Answer: 'No' (NDA required)
Avoma call 29 Apr 2026: Harold, 'is this a service, software, or tool that works with our current ERPs? Is this an outsourcing service?'
SF NextStep (5 Jun 2026): 'Conduct deep-dive discovery session: Current ERP and procurement tech stack' (competitive tech stack discovery pending)
competition.narrative: No competitor named by the buyer across any call or Salesforce field. SF Competitors__c is null. On the 23 May 2025 call, Gerardo Perez was asked 'Can you share with us who you are using for Source To Pay?' and replied 'No,' citing the absence of an NDA. On the 29 Apr 2026 call, Harold asked 'is this a service, software, or tool that works with our current ERPs? Is this an outsourcing service?' indicating Tekni-Plex may not have a Source-to-Pay incumbent or may be using fragmented/manual tools. Kevin Cipollaro referenced Ariba, Coupa, SAP, Oracle, and Workday generically as integration targets and competitive comparisons (e.g. 'Ariba got a negative 17, Coupa got an 11, Zycus got a 40' NPS scores; 'Panasonic transitioning from Ariba to Zycus'), but these were vendor landscape references, not confirmed competitors in this deal. The buyer's request for an NDA before disclosing their current tech stack and the absence of any 'we are also looking at X' statement suggest either (1) no active procurement-tech incumbent, (2) manual/Excel-based processes, or (3) a competitor exists but has not been disclosed. Competitive discovery is blocked by the NDA gate and is a priority topic for the deep-dive discovery session. If this is a greenfield procurement-automation opportunity with no entrenched vendor, that is a major wedge; if an incumbent exists, identifying it is critical.

identify_pain.narrative: Buyer pain centers on lack of visibility, manual procurement processes, cost-savings pressure, and need for data-driven negotiation and supplier management. On 29 Apr 2026 call, Kevin Cipollaro stated 'number one, visibility...I want to know where I need to consider from a risk standpoint...where am I spending? What could I be doing differently?' and the buyer did not object, indicating resonance. Carlos Velasquez confirmed 'benefits in savings that we can receive if we implement this software...that could be right now the priority that we have,' naming cost reduction as the operational pain driver. Harold asked 'what is the number one objective of your customers?' and accepted Kevin's answer (visibility/transparency first, savings second, efficiency third), suggesting Tekni-Plex shares that profile. Cristian Velasquez requested ROI examples for 'small, mid-size manufacturing companies,' implying Tekni-Plex sees itself as under-resourced or lacking procurement scale/sophistication relative to the 'humongous companies' Kevin cited (PwC, Panasonic). Carlos asked 'How the system knows all the specific details to compare apple with apples,' revealing frustration with current manual supplier comparison and category-specification complexity (e.g. chair materials: plastic, wood, steel, aluminum). Gerardo Perez (23 May 2025 call) asked 'How do you generate savings for the tail of your spend?' and 'How would you get generate savings?' indicating tail-spend visibility and sourcing efficiency are pain points. The pain owner is Cristian Velasquez (Sr Director Supply Chain), with Gerardo and Carlos as the day-to-day operators feeling the inefficiency.

paper_process.narrative: NDA is the immediate contracting step; MSA, SOW, legal review, and InfoSec are not yet in scope. On 29 Apr 2026 call, Cristian Velasquez stated 'what information...and Carlos, please make sure we have an NDA first' and later 'I can tell you that after the NDA's signature,' making the NDA the current gate before deeper discovery and data exchange. Kevin Cipollaro committed to sending the NDA immediately. Maureen Fitzpatrick (Sr. Corporate Paralegal) is listed in Contact Roles, indicating legal involvement is established. No discussion of MSA, Order Form, SOW, InfoSec review, or procurement-channel mechanics (e-procurement tool, PO process, supplier onboarding) has occurred; the deal is too early. Harold asked whether the solution requires 'spectral support for each location or one across all Techniplex,' hinting at deployment/contracting complexity across a multi-site organization, but no answer was recorded. The NextStep (5 Jun 2026) includes 'Share NDA with TekniPlex team (Cristian, Carlos, Gerardo) for signature' as the first action, followed by discovery and business-case build. Paper-process visibility will emerge in the deep-dive discovery session, which includes ERP integration inputs and data requirements—precursors to scoping the SOW and implementation plan.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer not yet identified. The 29 Apr 2026 call included Cristian Velasquez (Sr Director, Supply Chain FS Americas, tagged as Decision Maker in Contact Roles), Gerardo Perez (Procurement Manager, Evaluator), and Carlos Velasquez Garnica (Manager Procurement Capex, Evaluator). Commercial and pricing conversations have not occurred; the call was introductory and discovery-focused, with no discussion of budget authority, CFO involvement, or approval thresholds. Cristian asked about implementation value and data requirements but did not signal budget control. Harold (last name unknown, attendee on 29 Apr call) asked 'what is the number one objective of your customers adopting your software?' but provided no clues about his own budget role. The NDA request and discovery-first posture suggest procurement is still building the business case before engaging finance or executive sponsorship. Mapping the Economic Buyer—likely a CFO, VP Finance, or C-level sponsor given the $120k amount—is a critical next step once the value case is quantified.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is multi-step and procurement-led, but approval chain and timeline are not yet defined. The 29 Apr 2026 call sequence shows: (1) Gerardo Perez (Procurement Manager) initiated contact and attended intro call 23 May 2025, then requested a narrowed-scope follow-up; (2) Cristian Velasquez (Sr Director Supply Chain, Decision Maker per Contact Role) joined the 29 Apr 2026 call and took the lead, requesting NDA, discovery documentation, and a follow-up meeting with a solution consultant post-NDA; (3) Carlos Velasquez Garnica (Manager Procurement Capex, Evaluator) and Harold (role unclear) also participated. Harold asked whether Tekni-Plex needs 'spectral support for each location or one across all Techniplex,' indicating a decentralized org structure where deployment scope is a decision factor. Cristian stated 'I'll leave that especially to Carlos to keep this discussion with Gerardo and them,' delegating ongoing coordination to Carlos and Gerardo, but Cristian remains the decision authority. The process steps visible so far: intro/overview → NDA → discovery + data exchange → tailored business case → solution-consultant deep-dive → (inferred) module selection, pilot scope, implementation plan. No mention of CFO, legal beyond NDA, or executive-sponsor sign-off yet. The NextStep (5 Jun 2026) implies the next gate is the discovery session post-NDA, followed by business-case presentation. Approval thresholds, timing, and escalation path are unknown and must be mapped in the deep-dive session.

decision_criteria.narrative: Buyer has articulated initial evaluation criteria but not a formal scorecard. On 29 Apr 2026 call, Harold asked 'what is the number one objective of your customers adopting your software?' and Kevin Cipollaro (Zycus) replied visibility and transparency (priority 1), cost savings (priority 2), operational efficiency (priority 3). Carlos Velasquez confirmed 'benefits in savings' as the current priority. Cristian Velasquez requested examples tailored to 'small, mid-size manufacturing companies' to assess suitability and ROI, signaling that peer fit and use-case relevance are key criteria. Carlos asked detailed questions about supplier comparison logic ('how the system knows all the specific details to compare apple with apples'), indicating that data accuracy, benchmarking rigor, and intelligent matching are evaluation factors. Harold asked whether the solution is outsourcing, a service, or software, and whether it integrates with their current ERPs, marking integration ease and architecture as criteria. Gerardo Perez asked 'what is typically the basic agent that normally your customers require to understand about the tool' and 'what would be that cost for that basic system,' signaling that modular flexibility, cost transparency, and entry-point simplicity are decision factors. No formal RFP, RFI, or BRD has been shared; the deal is in discovery. The deep-dive discovery session documented in NextStep (ERP/tech stack, spend categories, supplier fragmentation, pain points) will surface the formal criteria.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Curious. Buyer is intellectually engaged with AI concepts (Carlos asked multiple detailed questions about how the AI agent works, Harold asked if it's a service vs. software, Cristian asked about implementation value) but has not articulated AI as a strategic priority or urgent need. No mention of AI/ML in their current tech stack or procurement strategy. The 23 May 2025 call (Gerardo Perez) and 29 Apr 2026 call show cost savings and process efficiency as the stated priorities, with AI as the means, not the end. Products field lists Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, indicating Zycus positioned AI-first, but the buyer's curiosity is pragmatic (will it work? how much will it save?) rather than visionary. No AIS score, status, or why populated in Salesforce. Tier: AI Curious (open to AI as a solution if ROI is proven, not AI-hungry or resistant).

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal stalled 29 days since last activity and 58 days since initial buyer call. Decision maker requested NDA and materials but has not re-engaged. No follow-up meeting scheduled despite explicit buyer ask for solution consultant deep-dive.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with strong product fit and engaged Decision Maker, but three critical levers must be activated in the next 30 days to maintain momentum and win probability: (1) deliver the overdue commitments (NDA, discovery doc, presentation, solution-consultant follow-up) to unblock buyer data disclosure and re-engage the thread, (2) convert the deep-dive discovery session into EB/CFO identification and champion development by asking who controls the $120k budget and who internally will drive adoption, and (3) secure peer-sized ROI proof and a quantified business case tailored to mid-size manufacturing to overcome the 'humongous company' objection and anchor the value story.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor disclosed. Buyer has not named an incumbent Source-to-Pay vendor or alternative being evaluated. Competitive discovery is blocked by NDA gate (required before buyer will disclose current tech stack per 23 May 2025 and 29 Apr 2026 calls). Buyer questions ('is this a service, software, or tool?', 'does it integrate with our ERPs?', 'what is the basic system your customers start with?') suggest possible greenfield opportunity with manual/Excel-based procurement or fragmented point solutions, not an entrenched enterprise S2P platform. If no incumbent exists, Zycus is the sole-source category creator; if an incumbent exists and is undisclosed, competitive positioning is blind. The deep-dive discovery session (NextStep, 5 Jun 2026) includes 'Current ERP and procurement tech stack' as a topic, making competitive discovery the immediate priority post-NDA.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong technical positioning, not yet buyer-anchored
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI positioning on paper (Merlin, ANA are the product scope per SF Products field) but not yet anchored to buyer's specific pain or ROI. Kevin Cipollaro led the 29 Apr 2026 call with Zycus' AI differentiation (20 years of Merlin development, autonomous negotiation, Gartner 'distinctive leader in AI'), and the buyer asked substantive questions about how the AI works (Carlos: 'How the system knows all the specific details to compare apple with apples?', 'is it a target point or looking for real information in the market?', 'is this to replace my agents or simplify their work?'). Kevin explained AI-driven benchmarking, market scraping, and autonomous negotiation, and Carlos accepted the answers without objection. However, Cristian Velasquez requested ROI proof for mid-size manufacturers, and Carlos asked about entry-point cost and modular flexibility, signaling that AI differentiation alone is not enough—buyer needs to see the AI translate to measurable savings and operational efficiency for their specific size and context. The tailored business case (NextStep, 5 Jun 2026) must quantify the AI value (e.g. '10-15% savings via autonomous tail-spend negotiation = $X for Tekni-Plex's $Y indirect spend baseline'). AI positioning is clear but not yet buyer-anchored.

items.quote: Carlos Velasquez: 'benefits in savings that we can receive if we implement this software...that could be right now the priority'; Cristian Velasquez: 'how does your company help companies my size...small, mid-size manufacturing companies'
items.criterion: Cost savings and ROI for mid-size manufacturers

items.quote: Kevin Cipollaro: 'number one, visibility...I want to know where I need to consider from a risk standpoint...where am I spending?'; buyer did not object
items.criterion: Visibility and transparency (top buyer priority per Kevin's assertion)

items.quote: Carlos Velasquez: 'How the system knows all the specific details to compare apple with apples and take the best decision?'; Kevin explained AI-driven benchmarking, market scraping, and criteria-based matching
items.criterion: Supplier comparison and benchmarking rigor (intelligent matching)

items.quote: Gerardo Perez: 'what is typically the basic agent that normally your customers require...what would be that cost for that basic system?'; Kevin confirmed modular approach and promised discovery documentation

Harold: 'is this a tool that works with our current ERPs?'; Carlos: 'we need spectral support for each location or one across all Techniplex?'; Kevin confirmed ERP integration and flexible deployment models
items.criterion: Integration with existing ERPs and multi-location deployment

items.quote: Cristian Velasquez: 'you are telling me examples of humongous companies'; requested examples for small, mid-size manufacturing companies
items.criterion: Proof via peer-sized case studies (not 'humongous companies')

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong initial fit on pain (visibility, savings, efficiency) and product scope (ANA, Spend Analysis). Buyer explicitly prioritizes cost savings, requested examples for mid-size manufacturers, and asked detailed questions about supplier comparison logic, modular flexibility, multi-location deployment, and entry-point simplicity—all areas where Zycus positioned strength. Fit concerns: buyer skepticism about 'humongous company' case studies (PwC, Panasonic) and need for peer-sized ROI proof; buyer caution around data requirements, integration complexity, and cost transparency. The tailored business case and discovery session (NextStep) are designed to close these fit gaps.

hard.ais_why:
• TekniPlex is in early stages of evaluating AI-driven procurement transformation with a focus on autonomous negotiation and AI-enabled sourcing. Direct match to ANA.
• Next step explicitly recommends an initial pilot scoped to Autonomous Negotiation on tail spend. Mutual alignment on agentic execution.
• Created late April 2026 with last activity mid-May and NDA workflow underway with Cristian, Carlos, and Gerardo. Active discovery cadence anchored to the AI use case.
hard.next_step:
• Share NDA with TekniPlex team (Cristian, Carlos, Gerardo) for signature.
  • Send Zycus presentation and overview materials.
  • Share implementation discovery checklist (data requirements, ERP integration inputs, supplier/spend data needs).
  • Schedule follow-up meeting with Solution Consultant (Karson) post-NDA.
  • Conduct deep-dive discovery session:
    • Current ERP and procurement tech stack
    • Spend categories (MRO, Capex, indirect)
    • Supplier base and fragmentation
    • Key pain points and improvement areas
  • Build tailored business case:
    • Savings potential (autonomous negotiation, tail spend, supplier consolidation)
    • Efficiency gains and cycle time reduction
  • Align on initial pilot scope (recommended: Autonomous Negotiation / tail spend category).
  • Define implementation roadmap and timeline (2–3 months for initial module).

deltas.subject: Close the delivery gap immediately to maintain buyer confidence and momentum. Four commitments made 29 Apr 2026 are now 58 days overdue: NDA, presentation slides, implementation discovery documentation, and solution-consultant follow-up. The buyer explicitly requested these before proceeding, and the NDA blocks tech-stack and competitive disclosure. Deliver all four within 7 days (by 3 Jul 2026) and re-engage Cristian and Carlos with a proposed date for the deep-dive discovery session. A 58-day silence after a strong buyer meeting is how deals cool; act now.

deltas.to: Build the peer-sized, quantified ROI case to overcome the 'humongous company' credibility gap. Cristian Velasquez said 'you are telling me examples of humongous
deltas.subject: Build the peer-sized, quantified ROI case to overcome the 'humongous company' credibility gap. Cristian Velasquez said 'you are telling me examples of humongous companies' and requested examples for small, mid-size manufacturers. Carlos confirmed savings is the priority. Kevin cited 10-15% savings but provided no Tekni-Plex-specific math. The NextStep includes 'Build tailored business case: Savings potential (autonomous negotiation, tail spend, supplier consolidation), Efficiency gains and cycle time reduction,' but it has not been delivered. Before the deep-dive session, prepare a one-page ROI model with peer benchmarks (2-3 mid-market manufacturing customers, ARR range $1-3M, 200-500 employees) showing savings %, cycle-time reduction, and payback period, and use the discovery session to validate assumptions against Tekni-Plex's spend data. This is the wedge that converts evaluation to commitment.
